articles.title: AlphaQuest LLC Buys 12,549 Shares of Ross Stores, Inc. $ROST
articles.summary: AlphaQuest LLC significantly increased its stake in Ross Stores during the fourth quarter, purchasing 12,549 additional shares to bring its total holdings to 22,465 shares valued at approximately $4.05 million. This increase follows strong quarterly results from Ross Stores, which reported EPS of $2.02 against expectations of $1.73 and revenue of $6.01 billion, up 20.6% year-over-year. The company also announced a quarterly dividend and maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $230.88.

articles.title: Allstate Corp Boosts Position in Costco Wholesale Corporation $COST
articles.summary: Allstate Corp significantly increased its stake in Costco Wholesale Corporation by 109.2% in the fourth quarter, now owning 33,975 shares valued at $29.29 million. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their holdings in Costco, which has an average analyst rating of "Moderate Buy" and a consensus price target of $1,052.60. The retailer recently reported strong quarterly earnings, exceeding analyst expectations, and increased its quarterly dividend.

articles.title: Truist Financial Maintains Target(TGT.US) With Hold Rating, Maintains Target Price $130
articles.summary: Truist Financial has reiterated its Hold rating on Target (TGT.US) stock, maintaining a target price of $130. This indicates the firm's view that the stock is currently trading at a fair value and does not recommend buying or selling at this time. The maintained target price suggests Truist Financial's continued outlook on Target's future performance.

articles.title: Target Corp stock (US87612E1064): Analyst upgrade and dividend yield attract fresh attention
articles.summary: Target Corp stock is gaining renewed investor attention following an analyst's price target increase to $114 and a robust dividend yield of about 3.6%. While a recent quarter was dubbed "Goldilocks," investors are keenly watching the sustainability of sales and margin trends. Target remains a bellwether for US consumer spending and a key dividend stock, navigating a competitive retail landscape.
